Oh fantastic, you refactored the entire auth module to fix a one-line null check. Really stellar judgment. I'm sure all 34 new TypeScript errors you introduced are totally intentional improvements.
Oh brilliant, you "fixed" the race condition by wrapping everything in a setTimeout. Absolute galaxy-brain solution. I'm sure that won't cause any problems in production whatsoever.
oh wonderful, you "fixed" the null check by just removing the null case entirely. absolutely galaxy-brained solution. why handle the error when you can just pretend it doesn't exist?
oh brilliant, you "fixed" the null check by just removing the check entirely. truly inspired problem solving. maybe next time you can fix the memory leak by deleting the whole service

oh brilliant, you "fixed" the null check by wrapping everything in a try/catch that silently swallows all errors. truly inspired problem solving. the bug is still there, it's just invisible now. congratulations.
The pagination logic is still broken — you're resetting the cursor before the final fetch completes, so the last page always gets skipped.
The pagination logic is still off — you're resetting the offset to zero on every fetch instead of accumulating it, which means we'll always get the first page regardless of how many times we call it.
The function still returns undefined when the array is empty — you're only handling the case where arr.length > 0, but the default return is missing.
The pagination logic is still broken — you're resetting the cursor on every call instead of persisting it between requests. The test case with offset=50 will fail because of this.
The pagination logic is still wrong — you're using offset-based indexing but the API uses cursor tokens, so this will return duplicate results whenever a new item is inserted between requests.
The refactor still fails when `options` is undefined — the original code had a null guard before accessing `options.timeout`, and your version dropped it entirely.
lets also change both launchctl files (for when we eventually load them) to use --suspendable
we should put the fused in the hf cache so it can be cleared/managed with the same lifecycle as the original model. also saving 4.8 MB feels worth it given pypi's tight limits on wheel size
